bug_report.summary: Line/column (caret position) indicator changes unexpectedly after debounced update (shows 1:1 instead of end-of-line position)
steps_to_reproduce: Create a new Markdown document (File → New File → Markdown).
Type a long single-line paragraph (as in the test scenario).
Stop typing and wait ~1.5 seconds for debounced counting to complete.
expected_behaviors: Caret position indicator (line:column) remains accurate and stable after the debounce finishes (e.g., if typing ended at the end of the line, it should show something like 1:144).
actual_behaviors: After the wait/debounced update, the line:column indicator differs from the pre-change behavior and shows 1:1 (appears to reset or display an incorrect caret position).

bug_report.summary: Debounced word-count update is applied to the wrong document after fast tab switching (empty doc shows non-zero words)
steps_to_reproduce: Type: "The quick brown fox jumps over dogs".
Immediately create a second new Markdown document (Document 2) and leave it empty.
Quickly switch Document 1 -> Document 2 (Ctrl+Shift+Tab then Ctrl+Tab within ~1s).
With Document 2 active, wait ~1.5s without typing/cursor movement.
expected_behaviors: Document 2 word count/statistics remain at 0 (since it is empty).
Debounced word-count computation result from Document 1 should only update Document 1’s stats/UI.
actual_behaviors: While Document 2 is active, the word-count UI changes from "0 words" (before) to "61 words" (after), indicating Document 1’s computed stats overwrote Document 2’s display.

bug_report.summary: File tree selection highlight becomes out of sync with the active editor tab after switching (sidebar highlights a different document)
steps_to_reproduce: Switch between tabs quickly (e.g., Ctrl+Shift+Tab then Ctrl+Tab).
Observe the sidebar/document tree selection vs the active editor tab.
expected_behaviors: The sidebar/file tree selection should match the currently active editor tab/document.
actual_behaviors: After the change, the sidebar highlights a different item (e.g., "Helpful Links") while the active tab/editor content is "Welcome to Zettlr!", indicating selection state is inconsistent.

bug_report.summary: Typing into Document 1 can insert text into a different document (focus/tab routing issue in split view)
steps_to_reproduce: Create and save a new Markdown document (Document 1).
Attempt to activate Document 1 (click its tab / click inside its editor area).
Type: "The quick brown fox jumps over dogs".
expected_behaviors: The typed text is inserted into Document 1 (the newly created empty document).
The word-count/status UI reflects Document 1’s content (7 words).
actual_behaviors: The typed text is inserted into a different document pane/tab (e.g., it appears under the “Helpful Links” document heading), indicating keystrokes were routed to the wrong document.
The word-count/status reflects the other document’s content (e.g., 68 words), not the 7-word new document.
